notes: Instruments page: add-funds, new-instrument, and unified history. (1) 'Add funds (invest)' action on investment cards opens the existing Journal invest modal pre-filled with that investment (source = savings) — a real investment_contrib transaction, so it ties to the projection (savings down, investment up, no double-count). (2) '+ New investment' and '+ New loan / EMI' buttons on the Instruments page create an instrument inline (editable immediately); add/remove no longer requires Edit my inputs. (3) Unified per-instrument HISTORY: each card now has a clean 'History · N' button (chips removed from the card face). The history modal merges BOTH lifecycle events (close/step/pause/resume — tagged 'Scheduled', deletable here) AND journal money-movements for that investment (invest/redeem — tagged 'Logged', view-only; manage from the Journal). Crucially this means a contribution or redemption made EITHER via the Journal page OR via the Instruments actions shows up in the same instrument's history, because both create the same underlying journal transaction that the history reads. Verified the merge filters by investmentId and sorts most-recent-first.
